I’ve got the current branch state. Next I’ll inspect the exact Lean diffs and theorem statements to verify whether prior feedback was actually resolved.

## Success criteria from issue description
1) Overflow bound is proved in `max_unreduced_additions_spec` (`proofs/Gf2Core/Proofs/MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean:791`).

2) Clamped value equality is proved in `max_unreduced_additions_value` (`...:875`), matching `k = min(u128::MAX / (P-1)^2, usize::MAX)`.

3) Unclamped division bound is proved in `max_unreduced_additions_div_bound` (`...:945`).

## Separation of concerns
Changes are confined to `proofs/` (`ModArith.lean`, `MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean`, `Progress.lean`). No `gf2-core` → `gf2-coding` dependency inversion.

## Functional paradigm and performance
Proof-only changes; no runtime kernels or GF(2) performance paths altered.

## Correctness
`p_minus_one_sq_le_u128_max` (`ModArith.lean:140`) correctly supplies the `(P-1)^2` fit bound used in progress/spec proofs. No BitVec/tail-mask invariants touched.

## Testing
Local `lake/cargo` execution was permission-blocked. Using provided gate state/history: `lake-build` passed; no fresh local lint/test evidence was obtainable.

## Unsafe isolation
No Rust `unsafe` introduced; all edits are Lean proofs.

## Documentation
Technical debt introduced: docstring for `max_unreduced_additions_div_bound` (`MontgomeryRoundtrip.lean:940-944`) claims returned-value equality `k = u128::MAX/(P-1)^2`, but theorem only proves positivity of `mp` and `(MAX/mp)*mp ≤ MAX`.

## Prior review feedback
Most recent run flagged theorem/documentation mismatch; this remains unresolved (same overclaim persists).

## Jit issue dependencies
`issue.dependencies` is empty; no prerequisite blockers.

VERDICT: FAIL

Gate result: FAILED
